parameters.content:
## Async AI

### How it works

This workflow exposes a chat completions-style webhook that can run either synchronously or asynchronously depending on whether callback configuration is included. It preprocesses file attachments and request metadata, then routes the request to an LLM provider. Asynchronous requests receive an immediate 202 response and later deliver results to a callback URL, while synchronous requests wait for the provider response and return it directly to the caller.

### Setup steps

- Configure the webhook URL and HTTP method expected by the chat completions client.
- Set up credentials or headers required by the configured LLM provider in the provider HTTP request nodes.
- Ensure the Prepare Data node builds the correct provider_config, callback_config, original_body, and job_id fields for your request format.
- If using asynchronous mode, make sure callers provide a reachable callback_config.url and any required callback method or authentication details.

### Customization

Adjust the provider URL, headers, and body mapping to match your chosen LLM API. You can also modify the attachment-processing code, callback payload format, or sync response structure to fit your client contract.

## Receive and prepare request

Accepts the incoming chat completions webhook request, processes any file attachments, normalizes key fields, and decides whether the request should run asynchronously with a callback or synchronously.

## Acknowledge async request

For callback-based requests, immediately returns a 202 Accepted response and removes callback details from the provider request payload before continuing in the background.

## Run async completion callback

Calls the configured LLM provider for the asynchronous path, enriches the completion with status and job metadata, then posts the result to the caller-provided callback URL.

## Run sync provider call

For requests without callback configuration, strips any callback-related data and sends the cleaned request directly to the configured LLM provider.

## Return sync response

Formats the synchronous provider output into the expected response body and status code, then sends it back through the original webhook response.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { "status": "accepted", "job_id": $json.job_id, "message": "Request accepted for async processing. Result will be delivered to your callback URL.", "model": $json.original_body.model } }}

parameters.jsCode:
const { callback, provider, ...clean } = $json.original_body;
return [{ json: clean }];

assignments.name: completion
assignments.value: ={{ (() => { const fallback = { error: { message: ($json.error?.message || "Upstream provider request failed"), details: ($json.error ?? $json) } }; const payload = $json.body ?? fallback; if (Array.isArray(payload)) return { data: payload }; if (payload && typeof payload === "object") return payload; return { value: payload }; })() }}

parameters.jsCode:
const { callback, provider, ...clean } = $json.original_body;
return [{ json: clean }];

assignments.name: response_body
assignments.value: ={{ (() => { const fallback = { error: { message: ($json.error?.message || "Upstream provider request failed"), details: ($json.error ?? $json) } }; const payload = $json.body ?? fallback; if (Array.isArray(payload)) return { data: payload }; if (payload && typeof payload === "object") return payload; return { value: payload }; })() }}
